hypothesis: If the integrated workflow is applied to Ca2SiO4 synthesis within the Ca–Si–O–C–N–H–Al–B space at 800–1600°C, then the identified synthesis recipes will be thermodynamically favorable (lower Gibbs free energy and higher likelihood of formation) and economically cost-effective (lower total reactant price at ≥95% purity) relative to alternative recipes, because SISSO-derived energetic descriptors, React-CA evolution, and supplier-feasibility data are combined to optimize both energy performance and cost.
